inspections.comments: Method for proper cooling 140?F to 70?F within 2 hours 70?F to 41?F with in 4 hours. I would suggest labeling the items being cooled with a date and time when the item started the cooling proces below 140?F. This will provide staff the information needed to properly cool the food item down to 41?F in the proper amount of time. Even if a mid-day staff change occurs.

violations.description: FOOD CONTACT SURFACES - CLEAN (2 pts): Observed utensils used in the hot held food items not being stored at or above 140?F utensils are not washed and sanitized at a proper interval.
FLOORS WALLS CEILINGS (1 pts): Observed vent covers in the dining room and the main kitchen noted not clean.

NON FOOD CONTACT SURFACES - DESIGN (1 pts): Service counter noted not in good repair. Observed the kitchen side of the food warming/serving window counter top to have grout lines and tiles in need of repair.

INSECT RODENT ANIMAL CONTROL (3 pts): The building containing the food service was noted with a structure which would allow the entrance of insects and/ or rodents. The rear storage room exit door sweep is missing allowing light to be visible under the door in turn could allow the entrance of insects and rodents (all of which are not present).
FOOD CONTACT SURFACES - CLEAN (2 pts): Cappuccino machine and juice machine have debris build up near interior dispensing chutes.

NON FOOD CONTACT SURFACES - DESIGN (1 pts): Service counter noted not in good repair. Observed the kitchen side of the food warming/serving window counter top to have grout lines and tiles in need of repair.
TOILET FACILITIES (1 pts): Adequate ventilation noted not present in the mens and womens toilet. The ventilation fan in both restrooms were not functioning.
